---
title: "A Guide to Nextflow & nf-core"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: night
    code-line-numbers: true
    highlight-style: monokai
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    scrollable: true
    controls: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: auto
    footer: "nf-core Hackathon 2026"
    logo: "https://nf-co.re/logo/nf-core-logo.svg"
    smaller: true
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-color: "#212529"
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Maven+Pro:wght@700&family=Inter&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
        <style>
          .reveal, .reveal-viewport { background-color: #495057 !important; }
          .reveal h1 { color: #24B064 !important; font-family: 'Maven Pro', sans-serif !important; font-size: 2.7em !important; }
          .reveal h2 { color: #24B064 !important; font-family: 'Maven Pro', sans-serif !important; font-size: 2.1em !important; }
          .reveal h3 { color: #24B064 !important; font-family: 'Maven Pro', sans-serif !important; font-size: 1.9em !important; }
          .reveal p, .reveal li { color: #f8f9fa !important; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif !important; }
          .reveal a { color: #24B064 !important; }
          .reveal .footer { color: #adb5bd !important; }
          .reveal .slide-number { color: #adb5bd !important; }
        </style>
execute:
  echo: false
  eval: true
---

## Congratulations! You Have 500 FASTQ Files 🎉

You just got **500 FASTQ files** from the sequencing run.

::: fragment
For each sample, you need to:

1.  Run quality control
2.  Trim adapters
3.  Align reads to a reference genome
4.  Call variants
5.  Generate a final report
:::

::: fragment
❓Are you going to write a **bash loop** and hope nothing crashes halfway through?
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## The Bash script worked .. until it didn't

``` bash
# The "it works on my machine" pipeline
for sample in data/*.fastq.gz; do
    fastqc $sample
    trimmomatic $sample ...
    bwa mem ref.fa $sample | samtools sort > ${sample}.bam
    # What if this fails at sample 247?
    # What about parallelization?
    # What about running this on a cluster?
done
```

::: {.fragment .fade-up}
🔴 No fault tolerance\
🔴 No parallelization\
🔴 Not reproducible\
🔴 Not portable
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## There has to be a better way ..

::: incremental
-   **Automates** orchestration of tasks and data dependencies
-   **Handles** compute resources across HPC, cloud, and local environments
-   **Recovers** from failures without restarting everything
-   **Scales** from laptop → cluster → cloud seamlessly
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## A Workflow Manager solves All of this ..

| Feature               | Why It Matters                        |
|-----------------------|---------------------------------------|
| 🛡️ Fault-tolerant     | Recovers from failures automatically  |
| ⚡ Parallelizable     | Runs independent tasks simultaneously |
| 🔁 Reproducible       | Same input → same output, every time  |
| 🌍 Portable           | Runs on laptop, HPC cluster, or cloud |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Meet Nextflow {background-color="#6c757d"}

A workflow manager **built for bioinformatics**!

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nextflow-io/trademark/master/nextflow-logo-bg-light.png)

::: incremental
-   Describes your pipeline as a series of connected steps
-   Built-in support for containers (Docker, Singularity)
-   Runs on local, HPC & Cloud
-   Backed by a dedicated bioinformatics community (nf-core)
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Nextflow vs The Rest

| Tool          | Strengths                   | Best For                  |
|---------------|-----------------------------|---------------------------|
| Nextflow  | Dataflow, containers, cloud | Bioinformatics pipelines  |
| Snakemake | Python-based, file-driven   | Python-centric teams      |
| WDL/CWL   | Specification specifications     | Platform interoperability |
| Airflow   | General-purpose, scheduling | Data engineering          |


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Let's dive into Nextflow

> 📚 Follow along: [training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Setting up your workspace

> 🔗 [training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/00_orientation/](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/00_orientation/)

### GitHub Codespaces ☁️

-   Just a browser + GitHub account
-   Pre-configured environment
-   ✅ **Recommended for beginners**

### Local Setup 💻

-   Nextflow installed
-   Docker installed
-   Code editor (VS Code)


------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Let's make sure everything is ready 🖥

``` bash
# Check Nextflow is installed
nextflow -version

# Check Docker is installed
docker --version
```

``` bash
# Explore the directory
ls -lathr
```

::: callout-tip
## Follow Along!

Your Codespace is already set up - just open the terminal and run these commands.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Part 1: Hello World - Let's walkthrough the Starter Script {.scrollable}

The `hello-world.nf` script is already in your Codespace current directory.
``` {.groovy filename="hello-world.nf" code-line-numbers="|3-6|8-14|17-21"}
#!/usr/bin/env nextflow

/*
 * Use echo to print 'Hello World!' to a file
 */
process sayHello {

    output:
    path 'output.txt'

    script:
    """
    echo 'Hello World!' > output.txt
    """
}

workflow {

    main:
    // emit a greeting
    sayHello()
}
```

::: fragment
💡 Two key blocks — `process` defines **what to run**, `workflow` defines **when to run it**.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Running Your First Workflow 🚀
``` bash
nextflow run hello-world.nf
```

::: fragment
``` bash
N E X T F L O W  ~  version 25.10.2

Launching `hello-world.nf` [goofy_torvalds] DSL2 - revision: c33d41f479

executor >  local (1)
[65/7be2fa] sayHello | 1 of 1 ✔
```
:::

::: fragment
💡 `[65/7be2fa]` is the path to the output in `work/` — every run gets its own unique directory!
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What's Inside `work/`? 📂
``` bash
work
└── 65
    └── 7be2fad5e71e5f49998f795677fd68
        ├── .command.sh    # ← the exact command that ran
        ├── .command.log   # ← full log output
        ├── .command.err   # ← any error messages
        ├── .exitcode      # ← 0 = success
        └── output.txt     # ← your result!
```

::: fragment
``` bash
# View hidden log files
tree -a work
```
:::

::: fragment
💡 `.command.sh` is your best friend when debugging — it shows exactly what Nextflow ran!
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Publishing Your Results 📁 {.scrollable}

Outputs are buried in `work/` by default — use the `output` block to save them somewhere accessible.
``` {.groovy filename="hello-world.nf" code-line-numbers="|5-6|9-14"}
workflow {

    main:
    sayHello()

    publish:
    first_output = sayHello.out    // ← assign a name to the output
}

output {
    first_output {
        path 'hello_world'         // ← save to results/hello_world/
        mode 'copy'                // ← copy instead of symlink
    }
}
```

::: fragment
💡 Default mode creates **symlinks** — if you delete `work/`, you lose the file. Use `mode 'copy'` to be safe!
:::

::: callout-warning
## Heads up — older pipelines use `publishDir`
``` groovy
process sayHello {
    publishDir 'results/hello_world', mode: 'copy'
    ...
}
```
You'll still see this pattern — but it will be **disallowed** in future Nextflow versions.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Variable Input via Command Line {.scrollable}

Three changes needed — add an `input` block, update the script, and set up `params`.
``` {.groovy filename="hello-world.nf" code-line-numbers="|2-3|7|11-13"}
process sayHello {

    input:
    val greeting                       // ← Step 1: accept a variable

    output:
    path 'output.txt'

    script:
    """
    echo '${greeting}' > output.txt    // ← Step 2: use the variable
    """
}

/*
 * Pipeline parameters
 */
params {
    input: String = 'Holà mundo!'      // ← Step 3: set a default value
}

workflow {
    main:
    sayHello(params.input)             // ← pass it to the process
}
```

::: fragment
``` bash
# Uses default value
nextflow run hello-world.nf

# Override from command line
nextflow run hello-world.nf --input 'Bonjour le monde!'
```
:::

::: fragment
💡 `--input` = pipeline parameter &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; `-resume` = Nextflow setting — note the difference in hyphens!
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Two Concepts. That's All you need.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {'edgeLabelBackground': '#6c757d', 'tertiaryTextColor': '#f8f9fa'}}}%%
graph LR
    A[Channel] -->|data| B[Process]
    B -->|data| C[Channel]
    C -->|data| D[Process]
    D -->|data| E[Channel]
    
    style A fill:#24B064,color:#f8f9fa
    style C fill:#24B064,color:#f8f9fa
    style E fill:#24B064,color:#f8f9fa
    style B fill:#212529,color:#f8f9fa
    style D fill:#212529,color:#f8f9fa
```

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### ⚙️ Process

-   A single step in your pipeline
-   Wraps a script (Bash, Python, R, etc.)
-   Defines inputs and outputs
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### 🔀 Channel

-   The pipe that connects your processes
-   Passes data between steps automatically
-   Drives the dataflow model
:::
:::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## The Superpower You Needed: `-resume` 🔄
``` bash
# First run — everything executes
nextflow run hello-world.nf

# Make a change, then rerun
nextflow run hello-world.nf -resume
```

::: fragment
``` bash
[skipped] sayHello | 1 of 1, cached ✅
```
:::

::: fragment
💡 Nextflow **hashes** inputs — if nothing changed, it skips the task!
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 🖥️ Your Turn: Run Hello World! 

``` bash
# Run the workflow
nextflow run hello-world.nf
```

::: callout-note
## Observe the output

-   What does the console show?
-   Where is `output.txt`?
-   What's in the `work/` directory?
:::

::: callout-tip
## Now try this

1.  Run with a custom greeting: `--input 'Bonjour!'`
2.  Make a change and rerun with `-resume`
3.  Check `results/hello_world/output.txt`

👉 [training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/01_hello_world](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/01_hello_world/)
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Part 2: Hello Channels - How Data flows through Channels

Channels are how data **flows between processes** in Nextflow.
``` groovy
// Instead of one greeting — feed multiple!
Channel.of('Hello', 'Bonjour', 'Hola')
```

::: fragment
💡 Each item in the channel runs the process **independently and in parallel**
:::

::: callout-tip
## 👉 Follow Along
[training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/02_hello_channels](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/02_hello_channels/)
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Part 3: Hello Workflow

Connect multiple processes — output of one becomes input of the next.
``` groovy
workflow {
    sayHello(Channel.of('Hello', 'Bonjour', 'Hola'))
    convertToUpper(sayHello.out)
}
```

::: fragment
💡 This is where Nextflow's **dataflow parallelism** really shines
:::

::: callout-tip
## 👉 Follow Along
[training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/03_hello_workflow](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/03_hello_workflow/)
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Part 4: Hello Modules 📦 

Modules let you **reuse processes** across pipelines — no copy-pasting.
``` groovy
// Import a process from a separate file
include { SAYHELLO } from './modules/sayhello.nf'
```

::: fragment
💡 This is the foundation of how **nf-core** shares tools across the community
:::

::: callout-tip
## 👉 Follow Along
[training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/04_hello_modules](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/04_hello_modules/)
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Part 5: Hello Containers

Each process can run in its **own container** — no more dependency conflicts.
``` groovy
process sayHello {
    container 'ubuntu:24.04'
    ...
}
```

::: fragment
💡 Same pipeline, same results — on any machine, any cluster, any cloud
:::

::: callout-tip
## 👉 Follow Along
[training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/05_hello_containers](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/05_hello_containers/)
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Part 6: Hello Config

`nextflow.config` controls how your pipeline runs — without touching the code.
``` groovy
profiles {
    docker { docker.enabled = true }
    slurm  { process.executor = 'slurm' }
}
```

::: fragment
💡 Switch from laptop → HPC → cloud by just changing the **profile**
:::

::: callout-tip
## 👉 Follow Along
[training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/06_hello_config](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/06_hello_config/)
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Welcome to nf-core {.center background-color="#212529"}

> From writing Nextflow → joining a global community

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What is nf-core? {background-color="#212529"}

:::::: columns
:::: {.column width="60%"}
::: incremental
-   🏠 A **community** of pipeline developers
-   📦 **90+ curated pipelines** (RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, sarek...)
-   🧱 **1200+ reusable modules**
-   🛠️ **CLI tools** for building & testing
-   📋 **Standards** for quality and reproducibility
:::
::::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nf-core/logos/master/nf-core-logos/nf-core-logo-darkbg.svg){fig-align="center" width="80%"}
:::
::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## How to run an nf-core pipeline

All nf-core pipelines follow the same pattern:
``` bash
nextflow run nf-core/ \
    -profile docker \
    --input samplesheet.csv \
    --outdir results
```

::: fragment
``` bash
# Always test first with the built-in test profile
nextflow run nf-core/ \
    -profile test,docker \
    --outdir results
```
:::

::: fragment
💡 Every nf-core pipeline has a `test` profile with small built-in data — no files needed!
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What is nf-core/demo? 🧪

A minimal nf-core pipeline built for **learning and testing**.
``` bash
# Run the demo pipeline
nextflow run nf-core/demo \
    -profile test,docker \
    -r dev \
    --outdir results
```

::: fragment
It runs three simple steps:

- `FASTQC` → quality control
- `SEQTK_TRIM` → adapter trimming  
- `MULTIQC` → summary report
:::

::: fragment
💡 Perfect for understanding how a real nf-core pipeline is structured — without the complexity!
:::

::: callout-tip
## 👉 Follow Along
[Run a demo pipeline](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nf-core/01_run_demo/)
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Reusable Building Blocks: nf-core Modules

Pre-built, tested, community-maintained **process wrappers**:
``` bash
# Search for a module
nf-core modules list remote | grep fastqc

# Install a module into your pipeline
nf-core modules install fastqc

# Check what was added
ls modules/nf-core/fastqc/
```

::: fragment
Each module comes with:

- ✅ Container definitions
- ✅ Conda environment
- ✅ Unit tests
- ✅ Meta map for sample tracking
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Your nf-core Swiss Army Knife 🔧 

``` {.bash code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-5|7-8|10-11"}
# Create a new pipeline from template
nf-core create

# Lint your pipeline for standards compliance
nf-core lint

# Launch a pipeline with interactive parameter selection
nf-core launch nf-core/rnaseq

# Download a pipeline for offline use
nf-core download nf-core/rnaseq
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## The nf-core Community 🌍 {.scrollable}

::: fragment
### Stay Connected
- 💬 **Slack** — [nf-co.re/join](https://nf-co.re/join/slack)
- 🐙 **GitHub** — [github.com/nf-core](https://github.com/nf-core)
- 🎥 **YouTube** — Weekly Bytesize talks
- 🦋 **Bluesky** — @nf-co.re
- 💼 **LinkedIn** — nf-core
:::

::: fragment
### nf-core Hackathon — March 2026 🎉
- 📅 **March 11–13, 2026**
- 🌐 Hybrid — in-person hubs + online
- 🗺️ **29 local sites across 20 countries**
- 🔬 **37 registered projects**

👉 [nf-co.re/events/2026/hackathon-march-2026](https://nf-co.re/events/2026/hackathon-march-2026)
:::

::: fragment
💡 You don't need to be an expert — every contribution counts and the community is here to help!
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## From Learner to Contributor

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {'edgeLabelBackground': '#6c757d', 'tertiaryTextColor': '#f8f9fa'}}}%%
graph LR
    A[Find Issue] --> B[Fork Repo]
    B --> C[Create Branch]
    C --> D[Make Changes]
    D --> E[nf-core lint]
    E --> F[Open PR]
    F --> G[Code Review]
    
    style A fill:#24B064,color:#fff
    style B fill:#396E35,color:#fff
    style C fill:#396E35,color:#fff
    style D fill:#396E35,color:#fff
    style E fill:#ECDC86,color:#3F2B29
    style F fill:#24B064,color:#fff
    style G fill:#24B064,color:#fff
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: fragment
### Where to look
- [github.com/nf-core](https://github.com/nf-core)
- Filter by `good first issue` label
- Check `documentation` labels
- Browse module requests
:::

::: fragment
### Great first contributions
- 📝 Fix documentation typos
- 📋 Improve parameter descriptions
- 🧪 Add test cases
- 🐛 Fix linting warnings
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 🖥️ Your First Real Contribution 

``` bash
# 1. Fork a repo on GitHub

# 2. Clone your fork
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USER/nf-core-REPO.git
cd nf-core-REPO

# 3. Create a branch
git checkout -b fix/my-first-contribution

# 4. Make changes, then lint
nf-core lint

# 5. Commit and push
git add .
git commit -m "Fix: description of change"
git push origin fix/my-first-contribution

# 6. Open a PR on GitHub!
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Keep These Handy {.smaller background-color="#343a40"}

:::::: columns
::: {.column width="33%"}
### Nextflow

``` bash
nextflow run main.nf
nextflow run main.nf -resume
nextflow log
nextflow clean -f
```
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
### nf-core

``` bash
nf-core list
nf-core lint
nf-core modules list remote
nf-core modules install <tool>
```
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
### Git

``` bash
git clone <url>
git checkout -b <branch>
git add . && git commit -m "msg"
git push origin <branch>
```
:::
::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Continue Your Journey {.center background-color="#343a40"}

| Resource | Link |
|------------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| 📚 Hello Nextflow Course | [training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/) |
| 🚀 Getting Started | [training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/00_orientation/](https://training.nextflow.io/latest/hello_nextflow/00_orientation/) |
| 🏠 nf-core Website | [nf-co.re](https://nf-co.re) |
| 💬 nf-core Slack | [nf-co.re/join](https://nf-co.re/join) |
| 🎥 Bytesize Talks | [nf-co.re/events](https://nf-co.re/events) |

------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 🎉 You went from "What is Nextflow?" to opening a real PR!
## Now, let's go build something amazing! 🚀
